findings.evidence: The PDF shows a centered NCDENR logo and three-column header with governor/director/secretary names aligned across the top. In the HTML this is flattened into plain stacked text with no logo and no three-column arrangement.

findings.description: Header graphic/logo is missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains the NCDENR logo at the top center and the 'One North Carolina Naturally' graphic near the bottom right. The HTML omits these graphics entirely.

findings.description: Text styling and alignment in the letter header are simplified.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses aligned TO/FROM/RE fields and visually distinct header formatting; the HTML presents these as running lines/stacked text with reduced alignment fidelity.

findings.description: Table structure is not faithfully preserved.
findings.evidence: The PDF page contains a two-column table under section A with headers 'Corps’ Regional General Permit Number' and 'DWQ General Certification Number'. In the HTML, although a table is present, the visible conversion shows row alignment problems and truncation/continuation issues at the bottom, reducing clarity compared with the compact intact PDF table.
The PDF uses indented lettered subsections and arrow-style bullets; the HTML converts these to generic bullets and paragraphs, making hierarchy less visually distinct though still readable.

findings.description: Bullet hierarchy and emphasis are partially flattened.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses arrow bullets, indented authority notes in brackets, and italicized condition labels; the HTML converts these mostly into plain bullets/paragraphs with less clear nesting.

findings.description: Dense condition text loses some visual hierarchy.
findings.evidence: The PDF distinguishes section labels and explanatory paragraphs with indentation and spacing; the HTML renders long condition blocks more uniformly, making subsections such as culvert placement requirements less visually separated.

findings.description: Indented subsections and bracketed notes are less distinct.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses clear indentation for 'Compensatory Mitigation' and other bullets, with bracketed editorial notes visually subordinate. The HTML keeps the content but with flatter spacing and hierarchy.

findings.description: Sub-item lettering and nested list structure are weakened.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents item D and E with nested lettered points (a-h) in a clearly structured layout. The HTML preserves text but reduces visual differentiation among levels.

findings.description: Final page bullet structure is simplified.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows arrow bullets and indented subpoints with a clean official memo structure. The HTML renders these as standard bullets/paragraphs, which slightly reduces scanability.

findings.description: The HTML does not preserve the document heading hierarchy using semantic heading tags. Section titles and document title are rendered as paragraphs with bold text instead of h1-h6 elements.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

North Carolina Department of Environment and Natural Resources Division of Water Quality

and

I. Umbrella Mitigation Banking Instrument (UMBI) Template for Nutrient Offset Mitigation Banks

rather than heading tags. The PDF clearly presents these as document title/header and section headings.

findings.description: Several text accuracy issues alter or degrade fidelity, including typos, punctuation changes, and OCR/conversion errors in names, citations, and quoted terms.
findings.evidence: Examples include HTML: "Lakes and Reservoirs, Wilmington" where the PDF shows "Lakes and Reservoirs,Wilmington"; HTML: "supercede" where PDF appears as "supercede"/standardization uncertain but fidelity is imperfect; HTML: "[Removed \"Trout\" waters" as they are not included in DLR's Sensitive Watershed Rules]" contains malformed quotation/punctuation not matching the PDF's bracketed note; HTML: "contractees" in section D likely mistranscribes the PDF wording; HTML adds a stray period in item h: "or."; HTML citation "[15A NCAC 07H .0205)]" has mismatched bracket/parenthesis.
